subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Sun: Our Nearest Star
description: A 60-second lesson on The Sun: Our Nearest Star.

hook.text: The Sun holds 99.86% of all mass in the solar system. Every planet and moon is a tiny leftover.

buildup.text: It's a yellow dwarf, 4.6 billion years old. Its core fuses hydrogen into helium at 15 million °C.

discovery.text: Each second, 600 million tons of hydrogen become helium. The lost mass converts to energy via E=mc².

twist.text: A photon born at the core takes 100,000 years to reach the surface—then only 8 minutes to hit Earth.

climax.text: In 5 billion years, the Sun will swell into a red giant, consuming Mercury and Venus.

punchline.text: One ordinary star. It runs everything we know.

quiz.question: How long does it take a photon to travel from the Sun's core to its surface?
